title: Binomial survival — multi-out / multi-street draw probability (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective)
bodyMarkdown:
Drawing-hand equity over multiple streets uses the binomial complement: P(at-least-one-hit) = 1 - P(no-hit-each-trial), where trials are sequential card reveals.

Flop-to-river formula with O outs and 47 unseen cards on flop, 46 on turn:
P(hit-by-river) = 1 - (47-O)/47 × (46-O)/46.

Worked examples (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective; outs counted vs current draw structure, no blocker adjustment):
- 9-out flush draw, flop-to-river: 1 - (38/47)(37/46) ≈ 35.0%.
- 8-out open-ender, flop-to-river: 1 - (39/47)(38/46) ≈ 31.5%.
- 4-out gutshot, flop-to-river: 1 - (43/47)(42/46) ≈ 16.5%.
- 15-out flush + open-ender (combo draw), flop-to-river: 1 - (32/47)(31/46) ≈ 54.1%.

Runner-runner (need both turn AND river to be specific cards):
P(both) = (out_turn / 47) × (out_river / 46).
- Backdoor flush (need 2 of suit on turn+river when holding 2 of suit on flop): 10/47 × 9/46 ≈ 4.2%.
- Backdoor straight (varies by gappedness): typically 1-3%.

Turn-to-river only (simpler binomial; 1 trial of 46 unseen):
P(hit-on-river) = O / 46.

Discount the raw binomial when blockers reduce the effective out count or when hitting still loses (reverse implied — see PRF-MATH-IMPLIED). See POKER_THEORY.md §6.6 (Combo Counting) for the unseen-card combinatorics underlying the formula.
theoryCitation: POKER_THEORY.md §6.6 (Combo Counting) — unseen-card combinatorics underlying multi-trial draw probability.

assumptions.field: all 9-handed live cash and tournament fields; binomial formula is field-invariant

atomicityJustification: Single derivation (binomial multi-trial draw probability) presented as one card.
